title: ctrl sub-barrel drift: root @rdna/ctrl barrel omits IconRadioGroup / NumberInput / ColorPicker / ProgressBar / Tooltip
summary: The root `@rdna/ctrl` barrel (packages/ctrl/index.ts) is out of sync with the per-area sub-barrels. selectors/index.ts also exports IconRadioGroup (lines 11-12) and the sub-barrels export NumberInput, ProgressBar, and Tooltip/TooltipProvider (controls/index.ts:8-9, readouts/index.ts:6-8). ColorPicker has a file (selectors/ColorPicker/ColorPicker.tsx) but is exported from neither. Consumers who import from `@rdna/ctrl` get a different component surface than consumers who import `@rdna/ctrl/selectors` / `/readouts`. This is a near-cycle / surface-drift risk rather than a runtime cycle — a future consumer adding `import { Tooltip } from '@rdna/ctrl'` will hit ambiguity with `@rdna/radiants`'s Tooltip. Not an import-graph cycle today, so classified as consolidation_candidate.
proposed_action: Make packages/ctrl/index.ts a thin re-export of the four sub-barrels (`export * from './primitives'`, `export * from './controls'`, `export * from './selectors'`, `export * from './readouts'`, `export * from './layout'`) and register ColorPicker in the selectors sub-barrel if it is intended to be public (or delete it if not). This eliminates the drift without introducing any new edges.

title: ctrl selectors/IconRadioGroup reaches across into readouts/Tooltip
summary: selectors/IconRadioGroup/IconRadioGroup.tsx line 6 does `import { Tooltip } from '../../readouts/Tooltip/Tooltip'`. No file in readouts/* imports anything under selectors/*, so there is no runtime cycle today. However, this is the only cross-subpackage leaf-to-leaf import inside ctrl, and it couples the selectors and readouts areas semantically — if readouts ever grows a dependency back on selectors (e.g. a Meter that shows a Tooltip-wrapped ChipTag), the cycle risk is immediate. Classified as consolidation_candidate, not a live cycle.
proposed_action: Move Tooltip/TooltipProvider from `packages/ctrl/readouts/Tooltip/` to a shared location such as `packages/ctrl/primitives/Tooltip/` (leaf, already imported by everything else) or `packages/ctrl/overlays/Tooltip/`. Update imports in readouts/index.ts and selectors/IconRadioGroup, and update the root index.ts to re-export from the new location. Keeps surface identical for consumers.

title: rad-os app catalog lazy-import pattern is safe today but fragile
summary: `lib/apps/catalog.tsx` uses `lazy(() => import('@/components/apps/BrandAssetsApp'))` etc. for 7 app components. Those same app components statically import `type AppProps` from `@/lib/apps` (barrel `export * from './catalog'`). Because React.lazy uses a dynamic import(), the static ES module graph does NOT cycle at runtime. This is not an actual cycle today. But: if anyone converts one of these lazy() calls to a plain static import (a common reflex when debugging a lazy-loaded component), it would instantly form the cycle `lib/apps/catalog.tsx -> components/apps/FooApp.tsx -> lib/apps/index.ts -> lib/apps/catalog.tsx`. Classified as consolidation_candidate — preventive barrel reshape, not a live cycle.
proposed_action: Split `AppProps` (and any other pure type exports) into a sibling file such as `apps/rad-os/lib/apps/types.ts` that has no runtime dependencies. Have the app components import from `@/lib/apps/types` instead of `@/lib/apps`. Keep the catalog barrel re-export as-is. This removes the fragile edge even if a future static import() creeps in.
